title: asperula aristata subsp. scabra
intro: Asperula aristata subsp. scabra, also known as Wood Sorrel or Woodland Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include astringent and antiseptic qualities, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Asperula aristata subsp. scabra include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as a tea or added to salads and soups, although it is essential to note that excessive consumption can cause gastrointestinal upset.
